_doc.what: Launch-time self-update manifest. operator dial/wiretap fetches this over HTTPS from raw.githubusercontent.com and, if a newer WHEEL is published, swaps just the wheel and re-execs. See src/_1_800_operator/pipeline/selfupdate.py.
_doc.release_process: On each CLI release: (1) tag vX.Y.Z, (2) optionally attach a prebuilt wheel asset + its sha256 under `wheel`, (3) set ref + components.wheel here on main. The client rolls forward to it on the next launch. Rollback = revert this file on main; the next launch rolls forward to the good version.
_doc.ref_rule: ref MUST be a vX.Y.Z tag or a 40-hex commit SHA. A commit SHA is recommended — it is immutable and defeats tag-movement.
_doc.wheel_field: Optional. When present as {"url": "https://github.com/.../releases/download/<tag>/<wheel>.whl", "sha256": "<hex>"} the client downloads + hash-verifies the wheel (byte-pinned). Absent → installs from the pinned git ref (identical trust to install.sh).
_doc.helper_aec3_rule: components.helper / components.aec3 are the operator version in which the Swift helper / aec3 binary LAST CHANGED. Bump them ONLY when that source actually changes — a wheel-only DOM fix must leave them untouched, or every hotfix would nag users to re-run install.sh. They must stay <= any installed wheel version so legacy installs (no ~/.operator/.components.json marker, which fall back to helper=aec3=wheel) are not falsely told to update. A helper/aec3 bump produces a log notice only; the heavy out-of-venv reinstall stays manual (install.sh / /operator:update) to avoid TCC prompts at launch.
